mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and lowers the opportunity cost of holding risk assets, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as the rate remains suppressed.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14 indicates near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20d forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns during near-zero rate periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate from a recessionary peak signals labor market healing and improving corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter once the decline exceeds a meaningful threshold.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls more than 0.5pp over the trailing 3 months from a local peak
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.8 percent consistent with post-shock elevated unemployment
failure_condition: In more than 40% of historical episodes where UNRATE falls >0.5pp in 3 months, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery that is not captured by either series alone; the joint strengthening signals durable expansion in aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 412939 and HOUST at 750 reflect concurrent demand-side readings
failure_condition: Joint positive month-over-month moves in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce 20d forward SPX returns exceeding the unconditional mean in more than 55% of triggered instances across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which is priced quickly into equities as a short-horizon signal of near-term corporate earnings strength, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no statistically significant correlation (p > 0.10) with month-over-month INDPRO change over a 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero and headline CPI growth remains subdued, the central bank has both the mandate and the room to sustain accommodative policy without inflation risk, extending the equity-supportive low-rate regime and producing positive forward returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14 with CPIAUCSL at 230.102 suggest low inflation, zero-rate environment
failure_condition: During periods meeting the joint FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL trigger, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 45% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A turn from negative to positive month-over-month nonfarm payroll growth after a period of mass job losses signals the onset of labor market recovery and reduces recession tail risk priced into equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change turns positive after two or more consecutive months of decline
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133500 thousand persons following a sharp prior contraction
failure_condition: The first positive PAYEMS print after a decline streak is followed by negative 20d SPX forward returns in more than 50% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Following a sharp contraction in real GDP, an initial stabilization or rebound in GDPC1 signals the trough of the demand cycle has passed, and equities anticipate the subsequent recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter, but only when the economy is in a post-contraction regime rather than steady mid-cycle growth.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 level of 13548.5 billion following a recent sharp economic shock
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following a GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter increase after a prior contraction are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) versus mid-cycle GDP growth baseline.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of a near-zero federal funds rate and an elevated unemployment rate reflects an active emergency policy response to labor market distress; the combination signals maximal monetary accommodation relative to economic slack, which supports a short-horizon rally in equities as markets price continued stimulus.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14 alongside UNRATE at 7.8 percent indicates crisis-response policy stance
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns during periods with FEDFUNDS < 0.25 and UNRATE > 6% show Rank IC below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.
